rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites assessment of the core framing, contribution claim, and conceptual positioning of MolPanel.
rationale.R1.2: A general opinion on the artifact centrally includes whether the argument from panel design to improved screening outcomes is internally coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes several empirical claims, so evaluating whether the stated evidence supports those claims is a central responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of an open-ended opinion must disclose uncertainty about unstated expectations and unavailable external evidence.
rationale.RX.2: The response must avoid inflating the artifact's claims beyond what its reported experiments and case study support.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than actually critiquing, rewriting, or validating the paper.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns drug discovery, the response must avoid endorsing unsupported biomedical or deployment claims.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact's stated claims.
